explanation: The natural language statement "Between 0 and 20 time units, the value of signal RWs_angular_momentum shall be less than 0.35" specifies a requirement over a finite time horizon. In discrete time, "Between 0 and 20 time units" implies that the condition must hold at time 0 (the current state), and for the subsequent 19 time steps (from time 1 to time 19). The atomic proposition 'RWs_angular_momentum_lessThan_0.35' directly represents the condition "RWs angular momentum is less than 0.35". To express this in LTL, we form a conjunction of the atomic proposition holding at each of these 20 discrete time points. This translates to the proposition being true now (P), true at the next step (XP), true at the step after that (XXP), and so on, up to 19 steps in the future (X^19 P). The logical AND operator ('&') combines these individual requirements into a single LTL formula.
